tool.name: WGCNA: Soft-Thresholding Power
tool.description: Estimate the soft-thresholding power for WGCNA network construction

configfiles.configfile:
library(WGCNA)
options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE) enableWGCNAThreads()
## Galaxy inputs counts_file <- "$counts_file" network_type <- "$network_type" variance_quantile <- $variance_quantile ## because both are declared with from_work_dir output_plot <- "softpower_plot.txt" output_conif <- "plots/softpower_config.png" ## Load counts counts <- read.delim( counts_file, check.names = FALSE, row.names = 1 ) cat("Counts loaded:", nrow(counts), "genes x", ncol(counts), "samples\n")
## ── Prepare expression matrix ──────────────────────────────────────────────── expr_data <- as.matrix(log2(counts + 1)) mode(expr_data) <- "numeric"
missing_values <- sum(is.na(expr_data)) if (missing_values > 0) { expr_data <- expr_data[complete.cases(expr_data), ] cat("Removed", missing_values, "missing values.\n") }
gene_variances <- apply(expr_data, 1, var) variance_threshold <- quantile(gene_variances, variance_quantile, na.rm = TRUE) filtered_expr_data <- expr_data[gene_variances > variance_threshold, ]
datExpr <- t(filtered_expr_data)
## ── Soft-thresholding ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── powers <- c(seq(1, 10, by = 1), seq(12, 20, by = 2)) sft <- pickSoftThreshold(datExpr, powerVector = powers, networkType = network_type, verbose = 3)
## ── Plot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── png(output_plot, width = 1200, height = 600, res = 100) par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
plot(sft[["fitIndices"]][, 1], -sign(sft[["fitIndices"]][, 3]) * sft[["fitIndices"]][, 2], type = "n", xlab = "Soft Threshold (power)", ylab = "Scale Free Topology Model Fit, signed R^2", main = "Scale Independence") text(sft[["fitIndices"]][, 1], -sign(sft[["fitIndices"]][, 3]) * sft[["fitIndices"]][, 2], labels = sft[["fitIndices"]][, 1], cex = 0.9, col = "red") abline(h = 0.85, col = "blue", lty = 2)
plot(sft[["fitIndices"]][, 1], sft[["fitIndices"]][, 5], type = "n", xlab = "Soft Threshold (power)", ylab = "Mean Connectivity", main = "Mean Connectivity") text(sft[["fitIndices"]][, 1], sft[["fitIndices"]][, 5], labels = sft[["fitIndices"]][, 1], cex = 0.9, col = "red")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1)) dev.off() cat("Soft-thresholding plot written to:", output_plot, "\n")
## ── Determine power ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── soft_power <- sft[["powerEstimate"]] ## 4 if (is.na(soft_power)) { soft_power <- 6 cat("No suitable power found. Defaulting to power =", soft_power, "\n") ## ignored } else { cat("Chosen soft-thresholding power:", soft_power, "\n") ##4 }
## ── Write softpower config ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── writeLines( c( paste0("soft_power = ", soft_power), paste0("network_type = ", network_type), paste0("variance_quantile = ", variance_quantile) ), output_config )
cat("softpower_config written to:", output_config, "\n")

param.name: counts_file
param.label: Raw counts matrix (TSV)
param.help: Genes as rows, samples as columns. First column is gene IDs.

param.name: variance_quantile
param.label: Variance filter quantile
param.help: Genes below this variance percentile are removed (default 0.4, bottom 40%).

tool.help:
**WGCNA Soft-Thresholding Power Estimation**
Runs ``pickSoftThreshold`` from the WGCNA package to estimate the optimal soft-thresholding power for scale-free network construction.
**Inputs**
- *Raw counts matrix* - TSV with genes as rows and samples as columns. - *Network type* - Signed networks preserve sign of correlations (recommended for RNA-seq). - *Variance quantile* - Filters low-variance genes. 0.4 removes the bottom 40%.
**Outputs** - *Soft-thresholding config* - Plain-text file with the chosen power value. Feed this into the **WGCNA: Full Pipeline** tool. - *Soft-thresholding plot* - Two-panel diagnostic (scale-free R² and mean connectivity vs. power).
